# =========================================================
# Screen count-matrix QC only
# =========================================================
#
# Main goals:
# 1) Read a pooled CRISPR screen count matrix
# 2) Compute raw-count QC metrics per sample
# 3) Add screen-specific QC:
#    - detected sgRNAs
#    - skew ratio
#    - simple CPM and TMM-normalized logCPM
#    - density plots
#    - PCA
#    - correlation heatmap
#    - pairwise scatterplots
#    - MDS
#    - MD plots
# =========================================================

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(edgeR)
  library(limma)
  library(ineq)
  library(ggrepel)
  library(pheatmap)
  library(stringr)
})
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 1. Paths
# =========================================================
input_file <- "input_files/Library_and_Screen.csv"
outdir <- "output_files/qc_screen_only"
dir.create(outdir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Optional control/QC files
essential_file <- "input_files/AchillesCommonEssentialControls.csv"
nontargeting_file <- "input_files/neg_controls_clean.txt"
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 2. Read count matrix
# Expected columns:
#   sgRNA or sgRNAID | Gene | sample1 | sample2 | ...
# =========================================================
count_matrix <- read.csv(input_file, check.names = FALSE)

if ("sgRNAID" %in% colnames(count_matrix) && !"sgRNA" %in% colnames(count_matrix)) {
  colnames(count_matrix)[colnames(count_matrix) == "sgRNAID"] <- "sgRNA"
}

stopifnot(all(c("sgRNA", "Gene") %in% colnames(count_matrix)))

meta_cols <- c("sgRNA", "Gene")
sample_cols <- setdiff(colnames(count_matrix), meta_cols)

meta_info <- count_matrix[, meta_cols, drop = FALSE]
raw_counts <- count_matrix[, sample_cols, drop = FALSE]

raw_counts[] <- lapply(raw_counts, as.numeric)

if (any(is.na(raw_counts))) {
  warning("Some count values are NA. Replacing NA with 0.")
  raw_counts[is.na(raw_counts)] <- 0
}

raw_counts_mat <- as.matrix(raw_counts)
rownames(raw_counts_mat) <- count_matrix$sgRNA
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 3. Define sample groups
# Adjust patterns if your naming differs
# =========================================================
sample_group_df <- tibble(
  Sample = sample_cols,
  Group = case_when(
    str_detect(sample_cols, "rep\\d*_counts") ~ "library_input",
    str_detect(sample_cols, "^Draxin") ~ "draxin",
    str_detect(sample_cols, "^Ctr") ~ "control",
    str_detect(sample_cols, "^Before") ~ "T0",
    TRUE ~ "unknown"
  )
)

group_colors <- c(
  "library_input" = "grey",
  "control" = "#6DAEB0",
  "draxin" = "#FF6666",
  "T0" = "black",
  "unknown" = "orange"
)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 4. Save clean raw count matrix
# =========================================================
raw_count_matrix_clean <- cbind(meta_info, raw_counts)

write.csv(
  raw_count_matrix_clean,
  file.path(outdir, "raw_count_matrix_clean.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE,
  quote = FALSE
)

# =========================================================
# 5. Raw-count QC summary
# =========================================================
raw_qc <- tibble(
  Sample = sample_cols,
  Group = sample_group_df$Group,
  Total_Reads = colSums(raw_counts, na.rm = TRUE),
  Detected_sgRNAs = colSums(raw_counts > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
  Percent_Zero = 100 * colMeans(raw_counts == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
  Percent_Low_Count_lt10 = 100 * colMeans(raw_counts < 10, na.rm = TRUE),
  Percent_Low_Count_lt30 = 100 * colMeans(raw_counts < 30, na.rm = TRUE),
  GiniIndex_Raw = sapply(raw_counts, gini)
)

write.table(
  raw_qc,
  file.path(outdir, "raw_count_qc_summary.tsv"),
  sep = "\t",
  row.names = FALSE,
  quote = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 6. Long-format counts
# =========================================================
long_counts <- count_matrix %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(sample_cols),
    names_to = "Sample",
    values_to = "Count"
  ) %>%
  left_join(sample_group_df, by = "Sample")

# =========================================================
# 7. Detected sgRNAs per sample
# =========================================================
detected_counts <- long_counts %>%
  filter(Count > 0) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Group) %>%
  summarise(Detected_sgRNAs = n(), .groups = "drop")

write.csv(
  detected_counts,
  file.path(outdir, "detected_sgRNAs_per_sample.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

p_detected <- ggplot(detected_counts, aes(x = Sample, y = Detected_sgRNAs, fill = Group)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Detected sgRNAs per sample",
    x = "Sample",
    y = "Number of detected sgRNAs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  file.path(outdir, "detected_sgRNAs_per_sample.pdf"),
  p_detected,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 8. Skew ratio per sample
# =========================================================
skew_ratios <- long_counts %>%
  group_by(Sample, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    percentile_90 = quantile(Count, 0.9, names = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    percentile_10 = quantile(Count, 0.1, names = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    skew_ratio_90_10 = ifelse(percentile_10 == 0, NA, percentile_90 / percentile_10),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.csv(
  skew_ratios,
  file.path(outdir, "skew_ratio_per_sample.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

p_skew <- ggplot(skew_ratios, aes(x = Sample, y = skew_ratio_90_10, fill = Group)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Skew ratio (90th / 10th percentile) per sample",
    x = "Sample",
    y = "Skew ratio"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  file.path(outdir, "skew_ratio_per_sample.pdf"),
  p_skew,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 9. Simple CPM normalization + log2 transform
# =========================================================
cpm_counts <- sweep(raw_counts, 2, colSums(raw_counts), FUN = "/") * 1e6
log_cpm_simple <- log2(cpm_counts + 1)

count_matrix_norm_simple <- cbind(meta_info, log_cpm_simple)

write.csv(
  count_matrix_norm_simple,
  file.path(outdir, "normalizedcountmatrix_simple_log2CPM.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# =========================================================
# 10. TMM normalization + logCPM
# =========================================================
dge_all <- DGEList(counts = raw_counts_mat)
dge_all <- calcNormFactors(dge_all)
log_cpm_tmm <- cpm(dge_all, log = TRUE)

log_cpm_tmm_df <- as.data.frame(log_cpm_tmm) %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "sgRNA") %>%
  left_join(meta_info, by = "sgRNA") %>%
  dplyr::select(sgRNA, Gene, all_of(sample_cols))

write.csv(
  log_cpm_tmm_df,
  file.path(outdir, "normalizedcountmatrix_TMM_logCPM.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 11. Density plots: TMM-normalized logCPM
# =========================================================
log_cpm_tmm_long <- log_cpm_tmm_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(sample_cols),
    names_to = "Sample",
    values_to = "logCPM"
  ) %>%
  left_join(sample_group_df, by = "Sample")

p_density_raw <- ggplot(log_cpm_tmm_long, aes(x = logCPM, color = Group, group = Sample)) +
  geom_density(linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "LogCPM density (TMM normalized, raw counts)",
    x = "log2-CPM",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave(
  file.path(outdir, "logCPM_density_raw_TMM.pdf"),
  p_density_raw,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 12. Normalized QC summary from simple log2(CPM + 1)
# =========================================================
qc_long <- count_matrix_norm_simple %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(sample_cols),
    names_to = "Sample",
    values_to = "Log2_CPM"
  ) %>%
  left_join(sample_group_df, by = "Sample")

qc_summary <- qc_long %>%
  group_by(Sample, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    Median_Log2_CPM = median(Log2_CPM, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_Log2_CPM = IQR(Log2_CPM, na.rm = TRUE),
    Percent_NearZero_CPM = 100 * mean(Log2_CPM < log2(5 + 1), na.rm = TRUE),
    Percent_Zero_CPM = 100 * mean(Log2_CPM == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    GiniIndex_CPM = gini(2^Log2_CPM - 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.table(
  qc_summary,
  file.path(outdir, "normalized_qc_summary.tsv"),
  sep = "\t",
  row.names = FALSE,
  quote = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 13. Combined per-sample QC summary
# =========================================================
combined_qc <- raw_qc %>%
  left_join(qc_summary, by = c("Sample", "Group")) %>%
  left_join(skew_ratios %>% dplyr::select(Sample, skew_ratio_90_10), by = "Sample")

write.csv(
  combined_qc,
  file.path(outdir, "combined_screen_qc_summary.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# =========================================================
# 14. Histograms of simple log2(CPM + 1)
# =========================================================
pdf(file.path(outdir, "histograms_simple_log2CPM.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)

for (sample in sample_cols) {
  p <- ggplot(count_matrix_norm_simple, aes(x = .data[[sample]])) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = paste("Histogram of", sample),
      x = "log2(CPM + 1)",
      y = "Number of sgRNAs"
    )
  print(p)
}

dev.off()
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 15. PCA on simple log2(CPM + 1)
# =========================================================
pca_input <- t(as.matrix(log_cpm_simple))
zero_var_cols <- apply(pca_input, 2, function(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0)
pca_input_filtered <- pca_input[, !zero_var_cols, drop = FALSE]

pca <- prcomp(pca_input_filtered, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca$x) %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "Sample") %>%
  left_join(sample_group_df, by = "Sample")

p_pca <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, label = Sample, color = Group)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA: log2(CPM + 1)",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(100 * summary(pca)$importance[2, 1], 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(100 * summary(pca)$importance[2, 2], 1), "%)")
  )

ggsave(
  file.path(outdir, "pca_plot_simple_log2CPM.pdf"),
  plot = p_pca,
  width = 7,
  height = 5
)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 16. Pearson correlation heatmap
# =========================================================
cor_matrix <- cor(log_cpm_simple, method = "pearson", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

write.table(
  cor_matrix,
  file.path(outdir, "sample_correlation_matrix.tsv"),
  sep = "\t",
  quote = FALSE
)

annotation_df <- data.frame(
  Group = sample_group_df$Group,
  row.names = sample_group_df$Sample
)

pdf(file.path(outdir, "sample_correlation_heatmap.pdf"), width = 6, height = 6)
pheatmap(
  cor_matrix,
  main = "Sample correlation (Pearson)",
  clustering_distance_rows = "correlation",
  clustering_distance_cols = "correlation",
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_format = "%.2f",
  color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "blue"))(50),
  annotation_col = annotation_df,
  annotation_row = annotation_df,
  annotation_colors = list(Group = group_colors)
)
dev.off()
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 17. Pairwise scatterplots
# =========================================================
if (length(sample_cols) >= 2) {
  combs <- combn(sample_cols, 2, simplify = FALSE)

  pdf(file.path(outdir, "sample_pairwise_scatterplots.pdf"), width = 6, height = 6)
  for (pair in combs) {
    df_plot <- tibble(
      x = log_cpm_simple[, pair[1]],
      y = log_cpm_simple[, pair[2]]
    )

    p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8) +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(
        title = paste(pair[1], "vs", pair[2]),
        x = paste0(pair[1], " log2(CPM + 1)"),
        y = paste0(pair[2], " log2(CPM + 1)")
      )

    print(p)
  }
  dev.off()
}
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 18. Boxplots of TMM-normalized logCPM
# =========================================================
group_factor <- factor(
  sample_group_df$Group[match(colnames(raw_counts_mat), sample_group_df$Sample)]
)

d_raw <- DGEList(counts = raw_counts_mat, group = group_factor)
d_raw <- calcNormFactors(d_raw)
logcpm_raw <- cpm(d_raw, log = TRUE)

pdf(file.path(outdir, "boxplots_raw_TMM_logCPM.pdf"), width = 10, height = 5)
boxplot(
  logcpm_raw,
  las = 2,
  pch = 19,
  col = group_colors[as.character(d_raw$samples$group)],
  outcex = 0.3,
  outcol = group_colors[as.character(d_raw$samples$group)],
  labels = colnames(logcpm_raw),
  main = "TMM-normalized raw data",
  ylab = "sgRNA representation (log2-CPM)"
)
legend(
  "topleft",
  legend = levels(factor(d_raw$samples$group)),
  col = group_colors[levels(factor(d_raw$samples$group))],
  pch = 19,
  cex = 0.6
)
dev.off()

# =========================================================
# 19. MDS plot
# =========================================================
selected_groups <- c("control", "draxin", "T0")
keep_mds <- sample_group_df$Group %in% selected_groups

pdf(file.path(outdir, "MDS_raw.pdf"), width = 6, height = 5)

if (sum(keep_mds) >= 2) {
  d_mds_raw <- d_raw[, keep_mds]
  d_mds_raw$samples$group <- droplevels(factor(sample_group_df$Group[keep_mds]))

  plotMDS(
    d_mds_raw,
    col = group_colors[as.character(d_mds_raw$samples$group)],
    pch = 16,
    cex = 1.8,
    main = "MDS: raw data"
  )
  legend(
    "topright",
    inset = c(-0.05, 0),
    legend = levels(d_mds_raw$samples$group),
    col = group_colors[levels(d_mds_raw$samples$group)],
    pch = 16,
    cex = 0.8,
    title = "Group"
  )
} else {
  plot.new()
  title("MDS: raw data\nnot enough selected samples")
}

dev.off()
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 20. MD plots per sample
# =========================================================
pdf(file.path(outdir, "MD_plots_raw.pdf"), width = 10, height = 12)
par(mfrow = c(ceiling(ncol(d_raw) / 2), 2))

for (i in seq_len(ncol(d_raw))) {
  plotMD(d_raw, column = i, main = colnames(d_raw)[i])
  abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
}

dev.off()
```



```{r}
# =========================================================
# 21. Essential-gene QC (optional)
# =========================================================
essential <- read.csv(essential_file, check.names = FALSE)

colnames(essential)[1] <- "Gene"
essential <- essential["Gene"]

# remove trailing " (number)"
essential$Gene <- sub("\\s*\\([^)]*\\)$", "", essential$Gene)

head(essential)

    essential_df <- log_cpm_tmm_df %>%
      mutate(essential = ifelse(Gene %in% essential$Gene, "yes", "no"))

    essential_long <- essential_df %>%
      pivot_longer(
        cols = all_of(sample_cols),
        names_to = "Sample",
        values_to = "logCPM"
      )

    p_essential <- ggplot(
      essential_long %>% filter(essential %in% c("yes", "no")),
      aes(x = logCPM, color = essential, group = essential)
    ) +
      geom_density(linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
      facet_wrap(~ Sample, scales = "free_y") +
      scale_color_manual(values = c("yes" = "#e41a1c", "no" = "#377eb8")) +
      labs(
        title = "Distribution of logCPM: essential vs non-essential guides",
        x = "log2-CPM (TMM normalized)",
        y = "Density",
        color = "Essential"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")

    ggsave(
      file.path(outdir, "essential_vs_nonessential_logCPM_density.pdf"),
      p_essential,
      width = 12,
      height = 8
    )
```
```{r}
# =========================================================
# Optional: Wilcoxon tests for essential vs non-essential
# =========================================================
essential_test_results <- lapply(sample_cols, function(cond) {
  group_yes <- log_cpm_tmm_df %>%
    filter(Gene %in% essential$Gene) %>%
    pull(all_of(cond))

  group_no <- log_cpm_tmm_df %>%
    filter(!Gene %in% essential$Gene) %>%
    pull(all_of(cond))

  wt <- wilcox.test(group_yes, group_no)

  tibble(
    Sample = cond,
    W = unname(wt$statistic),
    p_value = wt$p.value,
    median_essential = median(group_yes, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_nonessential = median(group_no, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

write.csv(
  essential_test_results,
  file.path(outdir, "essential_vs_nonessential_wilcoxon.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

wilcox.test(
  log_cpm_tmm_df %>% filter(Gene %in% essential$Gene) %>% pull(rep1_counts),
  log_cpm_tmm_df %>% filter(Gene %in% essential$Gene) %>% pull(Before1)
)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 22. Non-targeting QC (optional)
# =========================================================
if (file.exists(nontargeting_file)) {
  nontargeting <- tryCatch(
    read.table(nontargeting_file, header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (!is.null(nontargeting) && ncol(nontargeting) >= 1) {
    colnames(nontargeting)[1] <- "sgRNA"

    nt_df <- log_cpm_tmm_df %>%
      mutate(nontargeting = ifelse(sgRNA %in% nontargeting$sgRNA, "yes", "no"))

    nt_long <- nt_df %>%
      pivot_longer(
        cols = all_of(sample_cols),
        names_to = "Sample",
        values_to = "logCPM"
      )

    p_nt <- ggplot(
      nt_long %>% filter(nontargeting %in% c("yes", "no")),
      aes(x = logCPM, color = nontargeting, group = nontargeting)
    ) +
      geom_density(linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
      facet_wrap(~ Sample, scales = "free_y") +
      scale_color_manual(values = c("yes" = "#e41a1c", "no" = "#377eb8")) +
      labs(
        title = "Distribution of logCPM: non-targeting vs other guides",
        x = "log2-CPM (TMM normalized)",
        y = "Density",
        color = "Non-targeting"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")

    ggsave(
      file.path(outdir, "nontargeting_vs_other_logCPM_density.pdf"),
      p_nt,
      width = 12,
      height = 8
    )
  }
}

# =========================================================
# 23. Run summary
# =========================================================
cat("Screen QC complete.\n")
cat("Input file:", input_file, "\n")
cat("Number of sgRNAs:", nrow(raw_counts_mat), "\n")
cat("Number of samples:", ncol(raw_counts_mat), "\n")
cat("Outputs written to:", outdir, "\n")

```
